BUTTERNUT SQUASH RISOTTO

This creamy autumn dish originates from the Veneto region of northern Italy, where squash grows abundantly in the
cooler months. The natural sweetness of butternut squash pairs beautifully with the earthy undertones of sage and
parmesan. Italians have perfected this technique over centuries, building the creaminess gradually through patient
stirring and gradual stock addition. The result is a luxurious, velvety risotto that transforms humble ingredients
into restaurant-quality elegance. This recipe serves four as a main course or six as a generous starter.

INGREDIENTS:
400g butternut squash, peeled and cubed
1.2 liters vegetable or chicken stock, warmed
1 medium onion, finely diced
300g arborio rice
150ml dry white wine
100g parmesan cheese, grated
60g butter
4 fresh sage leaves
2 tablespoons olive oil
Salt and black pepper to taste

METHOD:
1. Heat olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat. Add diced onion and cook gently until soft
and translucent, about 5 minutes. Do not brown.

2. Toast the arborio rice in the pan for 2 minutes, stirring constantly. This helps release the starches and
creates a better texture.

3. Pour in white wine and stir until completely absorbed. Add sage leaves.

4. Begin adding warm stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently. Wait until each addition is nearly absorbed
before adding the next. This process takes approximately 18-20 minutes total.

5. When rice is almost tender, add the diced butternut squash. Continue stirring and adding stock as needed.
The squash should soften after about 6-8 minutes.

6. Taste the rice. It should be creamy yet retain a slight firmness (al dente).

7. Remove from heat. Stir in butter and grated parmesan. Season with salt and pepper. Let rest 1-2 minutes.

8. Serve immediately with additional parmesan and a drizzle of excellent olive oil.

NOTES: For deeper flavor, roast the squash before adding. Some chefs finish with a knob of cold butter for
extra richness. Wine can be replaced with stock if preferred.
